segments.text: This is the word that the LORD spoke through Jeremiah the prophet concerning Babylon and the land of the Chaldeans:

segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For a nation from the north will come against her; it will make her land a desolation. No one will live in it; both man and beast will flee.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
In those days and at that time, declares the LORD, the children of Israel and the children of Judahwill come together, weeping as they come, 
and will seek the LORD their God.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They will ask the way to Zion and turn their faces toward it. They will come and join themselves to the LORDin an everlasting covenant
that will never be forgotten.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
My people are lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray, 
causing them to roam the mountains. They have wandered from mountain to hill; they have forgotten their resting place.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
All who found them devoured them, and their enemies said,‘We are not guilty, for they have sinned against the LORD, their true pasture, 
the LORD, the hope of their fathers.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Flee from the midst of Babylon; depart from the land of the Chaldeans; 
be like the he-goats that lead the flock.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For behold, I stir up and bring against Babylonan assembly of great nations from the land of the north. They will line up against her; from the north she will be captured. Their arrows will be like skilled warriors who do not return empty-handed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Chaldea will be plundered; all who plunder her will have their fill,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: declares the LORD.

Because you rejoice, because you sing in triumph—
you who plunder My inheritance—because you frolic like a heifer treading grain and neigh like stallions,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: your mother will be greatly ashamed; she who bore you will be disgraced. Behold, she will be the least of the nations, a wilderness, a dry land, and a desert.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Because of the wrath of the LORD, she will not be inhabited; 
she will become completely desolate. All who pass through Babylon will be horrified and will hiss at all her wounds.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Line up in formation around Babylon, all you who draw the bow! Shoot at her! Spare no arrows! For she has sinned against the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Raise a war cry against her on every side! She has thrown up her hands in surrender; her towers have fallen; her walls are torn down. 
Since this is the vengeance of the LORD, take out your vengeance upon her; as she has done, do the same to her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Cut off the sower from Babylon, and the one who wields the sickle at harvest time. In the face of the oppressor’s sword, each will turn to his own people, 
each will flee to his own land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Israel is a scattered flock, chased away by lions. The first to devour himwas the king of Assyria; the last to crush his boneswas Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore this is what the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
I will punish the king of Babylon and his landas I punished the king of Assyria.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will return Israel to his pasture, and he will graze on Carmel and Bashan; his soul will be satisfiedon the hills of Ephraim and Gilead.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In those days and at that time, declares the LORD, a search will be made for Israel’s guilt, but there will be none, and for Judah’s sins, but they will not be found; for I will forgivethe remnant I preserve.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Go up against the land of Merathaim, and against the residents of Pekod. Kill them and devote them to destruction.  Do all that I have commanded you,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: declares the LORD.
The noise of battle is in the land—the noise of great destruction.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: How the hammer of the whole earthlies broken and shattered! What a horror Babylon has becomeamong the nations!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I laid a snare for you, O Babylon, and you were caught before you knew it. You were found and capturedbecause you challenged the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The LORD has opened His armory and brought out His weapons of wrath, for this is the work of the Lord GOD of Hostsin the land of the Chaldeans.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Come against herfrom the farthest border. Break open her granaries; pile her up like mounds of grain. Devote her to destruction; leave her no survivors.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Kill all her young bulls; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them, for their day has come—the time of their punishment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Listen to the fugitives and refugeesfrom the land of Babylon, declaring in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God, the vengeance for His temple.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Summon the archers against Babylon, all who string the bow. Encamp all around her; let no one escape. Repay her according to her deeds; do to her as she has done. For she has defied the LORD, the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Therefore, her young men will fall in the streets, and all her warriors will be silenced in that day,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: declares the LORD.
Behold, I am against you, O arrogant one,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: declares the Lord GOD of Hosts,
for your day has come, the time when I will punish you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The arrogant one will stumble and fallwith no one to pick him up. And I will kindle a fire in his citiesto consume all those around him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is what the LORD of Hosts says:
The sons of Israel are oppressed, and the sons of Judah as well. All their captors hold them fast, refusing to release them.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text:
Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of Hosts is His name. He will fervently plead their caseso that He may bring rest to the earth, 
but turmoil to those who live in Babylon.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: A sword is against the Chaldeans, declares the LORD, against those who live in Babylon, and against her officials and wise men.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: A sword is against her false prophets, and they will become fools. A sword is against her warriors, and they will be filled with terror.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text:
A sword is against her horses and chariots and against all the foreigners in her midst, 
and they will become like women. A sword is against her treasuries, and they will be plundered.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: A drought is upon her waters, and they will be dried up. For it is a land of graven images, and the people go mad over idols.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: So the desert creatures and hyenas will live there and ostriches will dwell there. It will never again be inhabitedor lived in from generation to generation.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: As God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrahalong with their neighbors,
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: declares the LORD,
no one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text:
Behold, an army is coming from the north; a great nation and many kings are stirred up
from the ends of the earth.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: They grasp the bow and spear;  they are cruel and merciless. Their voice roars like the sea, and they ride upon horses, lined up like men in formationagainst you, O Daughter of Babylon.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: The king of Babylon has heard the report, and his hands hang limp. Anguish has gripped him, pain like that of a woman in labor.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Behold, one will come up like a lionfrom the thickets of the Jordan to the watered pasture. For in an instant I will chase Babylon from her land. Who is the chosen one I will appoint for this? For who is like Me, and who can challenge Me? What shepherd can st and against Me?
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: Therefore hear the plansthat the LORD has drawn up against Babylon and the strategies He has devisedagainst the land of the Chaldeans: Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them.
At the sound of Babylon’s capture the earth will quake; a cry will be heard among the nations.
